title: Implement Multi-Tenant Account Foundation with User Onboarding and Profile Management
body:
## User Story

As a platform administrator, I want Concoction to support accounts, invitations, and user profiles so that teams can securely collaborate inside shared synthetic data environments.

## Business Requirements

- Support account as the primary tenant boundary.
- Allow users to create an account or join via invitation.
- Provide personal profile management.
- Support Owner and Member roles in MVP.
- Keep account administration separate from personal settings.

## Technical Requirements

- Add entities: Account, AccountMembership, UserProfile, Invitation, AccountRole.
- Add services for create account, invite user, accept/revoke invite, and update profile.
- Define `IAccountService`, `IInvitationService`, and `IUserProfileService` application ports.
- Register persistence and audit adapters in the DI composition root.
- Enforce tenant isolation for all account-scoped operations.
- Persist invitation lifecycle metadata (issuer, email, expiry, status).
- Emit auditable events for onboarding/profile actions.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] New account creator is assigned Owner role automatically.
- [ ] Owner can invite users and invited users can join as Member.
- [ ] Expired/revoked invitations are rejected.
- [ ] Users can update profile without account-admin permission.
- [ ] Cross-tenant access is blocked.
- [ ] Audit records exist for account/profile lifecycle actions.
- [ ] Validation uses production-like persistence/infrastructure in integration tests.
- [ ] Unauthorized users cannot access invitation management endpoints.
- [ ] Invalid invitation tokens are rejected with actionable errors.
- [ ] Cross-tenant data-leakage attempts are blocked and captured in audit records.

title: Add Account Governance for Roles, Allowed Domains, Groups, and Audit Logs
body:
## User Story

As an account owner, I want to manage roles, domains, groups, and audit logs so that Concoction remains secure and compliant.

## Business Requirements

- Owners can manage users and roles.
- Owners can manage allowed email domains.
- Support account groups for bulk workspace permission assignment.
- Support searchable/exportable audit logs with retention controls.

## Technical Requirements

- Add account group and group-membership models.
- Enforce allowed-domain policy in invitation/join flow.
- Add immutable audit events with actor/target/timestamp/correlation id.
- Provide paginated/filterable audit queries.
- Prevent sensitive payloads from being stored in logs.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Only Owners can change governance settings.
- [ ] Invitations/joins from disallowed domains are denied.
- [ ] Group-based permission inheritance works as designed.
- [ ] Governance changes are captured in audit logs.
- [ ] Audit export omits secrets/sensitive values.
- [ ] Retention policy is enforced.
- [ ] Governance behavior is validated with full authorization and storage integration coverage.
- [ ] Negative authorization tests verify non-Owner governance changes are denied.
- [ ] Audit log tampering/injection attempts are rejected and recorded.
- [ ] Tests assert PII/secrets are absent from audit export outputs.

title: Build Workspace Management with Scoped Access, Connection Catalog, Secrets, and Agent Instructions
body:
## User Story

As a workspace owner, I want to manage access, approved connections, secrets, and instructions so that teams can safely configure generation environments.

## Business Requirements

- Support one or more workspaces per account.
- Support workspace-scoped RBAC for users/groups.
- Support workspace connection catalog.
- Support reusable secret references.
- Support versioned workspace agent instructions.

## Technical Requirements

- Add models for Workspace, membership/roles, connections, secret refs, instruction versions.
- Integrate with a secure secret provider adapter for secret values.
- Define an `ISecretProvider` application port and register infrastructure adapters in the DI composition root.
- Enforce workspace authorization policies.
- Store instruction history and change metadata.
- Emit audit/telemetry on configuration changes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Workspace lifecycle operations are role-protected.
- [ ] User/group workspace assignments are enforced.
- [ ] Connections can be validated, enabled/disabled, and removed.
- [ ] Secrets are never exposed in logs/API payloads.
- [ ] Instruction versioning is queryable and auditable.
- [ ] Workspace isolation is enforced.
- [ ] End-to-end validation uses integrated secret/provider adapters in controlled test environments.
- [ ] Negative tests verify unauthorized secret access is blocked.
- [ ] Tests confirm secrets are absent from error responses and audit logs.

title: Deliver Project Center with Project Lifecycle, Database Catalog, Local Databases, and External Connections
body:
## User Story

As a data engineer, I want project-centric organization of local and external databases so that generation work maps to real application boundaries.

## Business Requirements

- Multiple projects per workspace.
- Unified database catalog per project.
- Project lifecycle management (create/rename/archive/delete).
- Manage local synthetic databases and external bindings.

## Technical Requirements

- Add Project and ProjectDatabase models.
- Normalize catalog metadata (type/provider/status/ownership).
- Preserve lineage with archive/soft-delete behavior.
- Authorize all operations via workspace/project RBAC.
- Define `IProjectService` and `IProjectDatabaseCatalog` application ports.
- Register metadata and authorization adapters via DI in the composition root.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Authorized users can manage project lifecycle.
- [ ] Projects support both local and external databases.
- [ ] Catalog clearly identifies source type/provider/status.
- [ ] Archiving preserves lineage and run history.
- [ ] Unauthorized operations are blocked.
- [ ] Integration validation uses full metadata/storage layers.
- [ ] Negative tests verify unauthorized project access/modification is blocked.
- [ ] Cross-workspace project enumeration attempts are rejected.

title: Implement Schema Import, Safe Profiling, and Source-Data Review Workflow
body:
## User Story

As a data steward, I want Concoction to import schema and profile safely so that generated data remains realistic without exposing sensitive source data.

## Business Requirements

- Import schema from live connections and files.
- Capture aggregate profiling statistics (not raw sensitive data by default).
- Provide review/diagnostic workflow before generation.
- Reuse schema/profile snapshots in planning.

## Technical Requirements

- Define or extend `ISchemaProvider` port for both connection and file ingestion operations.
- Extend schema-provider adapters for connection + file ingestion.
- Register database/file schema adapters in the DI composition root.
- Build canonical metadata/profiling model.
- Add diagnostics for unsupported/partial metadata.
- Version schema/profile snapshots.
- Enforce privacy policy during profiling and review.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Live connection import returns canonical schema model.
- [ ] File import returns equivalent canonical schema model.
- [ ] Profiling persists aggregate-only stats by default.
- [ ] Review step surfaces schema graph and diagnostics.
- [ ] Unsupported metadata yields actionable warnings/errors.
- [ ] Profile versions are selectable for plan creation.
- [ ] Integration validation runs against real database engines in sandboxed environments.

title: Build Generation Plan Engine with Canonical Data Kinds and Transformation Strategy Resolution
body:
## User Story

As a synthetic data engineer, I want automatic strategy resolution with override controls so that I can generate realistic data with predictable behavior.

## Business Requirements

- Map source fields to canonical data kinds.
- Support scoped override precedence (global/project/table/column).
- Support transformations: synthetic generation, mask, hash/tokenize, null, preserve-format, lookup/reference, distribution sampling, semantic text generation.
- Provide plan transparency before execution.

## Technical Requirements

- Extend DataKind model and strategy registry.
- Define `IGenerationPlanService` and `IStrategyRegistry` application ports.
- Register strategy resolution and plan persistence adapters via DI in the composition root.
- Implement deterministic precedence/merge rules.
- Support nested JSON/XML path-level strategies.
- Persist strategy provenance and diagnostics per plan.
- Support deterministic seed replay.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Type/data-kind mapping is correct for supported providers.
- [ ] Override precedence behaves deterministically.
- [ ] Nested structures support path-level strategy assignment.
- [ ] Ambiguous mappings fall back safely with diagnostics.
- [ ] Effective plan is reviewable before run.
- [ ] Same seed + same plan is reproducible.
- [ ] Plan behavior validated with full generation pipeline components.

title: Implement Referentially Intact Synthetic Generation with Compliance-Safe Transformations
body:
## User Story

As a QA lead, I want realistic compliant datasets with preserved relationships so that systems can be tested safely and accurately.

## Business Requirements

- Preserve FK integrity across full dataset.
- Support acyclic, self-referencing, and cyclic schemas.
- Enforce GDPR/HIPAA-oriented sensitive-field policies.
- Honor nullability, uniqueness, ranges, and cardinality.
- Enforce guardrails on semantic generation.

## Technical Requirements

- Define `IReferentialIntegrityPlanner` and `ISensitiveFieldPolicy` application ports.
- Dependency graph planning + cycle backfill handling.
- Register dependency-graph, key-pool, and compliance-policy adapters via DI in the composition root.
- FK key-pool/reference sampling.
- Sensitive-field classification + strategy policy enforcement.
- Profile-based compliance modes (default/healthcare/finance).
- Persist field-level strategy provenance and validation outcomes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Required FK integrity violations are zero.
- [ ] Cycles resolve to valid final relational state.
- [ ] Sensitive fields are transformed per active policy.
- [ ] Disallowed policy combinations are blocked.
- [ ] Semantic outputs pass policy/schema guardrails.
- [ ] Run metadata includes provenance and compliance outputs.
- [ ] Integrity/compliance validated with real DB constraint enforcement in integration tests.
- [ ] Negative tests verify disallowed sensitive-field strategies are rejected before generation starts.
- [ ] Policy violations fail with actionable error messages for GDPR/HIPAA compliance checks.

title: Build Data Agent Chat with Sessions, Modes, and Instruction Management
body:
## User Story

As a product engineer, I want a Data Agent chat that manages real generation workflows so that I can define and refine datasets conversationally.

## Business Requirements

- Chat session lifecycle (create/resume/rename/archive).
- Clarifying-question behavior for incomplete requests.
- Configurable run modes (guided/autonomous/review-required).
- Layered instruction model (workspace/project/chat override).
- Auditable tool-invocation and decision history.

## Technical Requirements

- Add chat, message, instruction, and invocation models.
- Implement instruction composition and precedence.
- Support structured tool calls for discovery/profiling/planning/generation/validation/export.
- Define an `ITool` port interface with allowlist-based registration in the tool registry.
- Enforce least-privilege tool access using workspace/project-scoped permissions.
- Persist sessions and plan revisions.
- Enforce authorization and sanitize logs.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Session lifecycle is supported and role-protected.
- [ ] Agent asks clarifying questions when needed.
- [ ] Mode changes alter approval/execution behavior correctly.
- [ ] Instruction inheritance and overrides behave correctly.
- [ ] Tool calls and plan revisions are auditable.
- [ ] Cross-workspace access is blocked.
- [ ] Workflow validated against full orchestration and persistence components.
- [ ] Negative tests verify unauthorized tool invocations are blocked and audited.
- [ ] Out-of-scope workspace/project resource access via tool calls is rejected.

title: Deliver Workflow Orchestration, Custom Skills, and Production API Endpoint Generation
body:
## User Story

As an engineering team, I want reusable workflows, custom skills, and production API endpoint generation so that I can automate synthetic data operations and validate real integrations.

## Business Requirements

- Create/version/execute reusable workflows.
- Support custom skills with scoped permissions and governance.
- Generate production-ready API endpoints from schema/OpenAPI contracts.
- Ensure endpoint responses are backed by actual generated datasets.
- Track workflow/skill/endpoint changes in audit and lineage.

## Technical Requirements

- Add workflow definition/execution models with step-level statuses.
- Implement workspace-scoped skill registry with allowlisted tool access.
- Add API contract ingestion (OpenAPI/Swagger) and endpoint generation pipeline.
- Bind generated endpoints to full data stores/artifacts.
- Implement endpoint auth, rate limiting, observability, and error handling.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Users can create, version, run, and disable workflows.
- [ ] Workflow runs expose step progress, retries, and failures.
- [ ] Skills are permission-scoped and governed.
- [ ] Endpoint generation from supported API contracts succeeds.
- [ ] Generated endpoints return contract-valid payloads from generated datasets.
- [ ] Endpoint security, telemetry, and audit records are present.
- [ ] Validation includes end-to-end calls against generated live endpoints in integration environments.
- [ ] Negative authorization tests confirm unauthorized skill execution is blocked.
- [ ] Rate-limiting behavior is validated under load and enforced at runtime.
- [ ] Security tests confirm endpoint auth rejects invalid/expired credentials with consistent errors.

title: Implement Dataset Run Management, Validation, Export, and Delivery
body:
## User Story

As a release engineer, I want validated exportable synthetic datasets with full run history so that teams can reliably consume them in CI/test/demo environments.

## Business Requirements

- End-to-end run lifecycle from import/review to export.
- Validation for integrity, quality, and distribution drift.
- MVP exports: SQL, CSV, JSON.
- Run manifest with lineage, seed, schema/profile versions, and checksums.
- Retrieval of artifacts and run history.

## Technical Requirements

- Run state model with queued/running/completed/failed/cancelled.
- Define `IConstraintEvaluator`, `IExporter`, and `IArtifactStore` application ports.
- Validation engine for FK/nullability/uniqueness/type/distribution checks.
- Export adapters and artifact storage abstraction.
- Register validation, export-format, and artifact-storage adapters in the DI composition root.
- Manifest generation with reproducibility metadata.
- Policy-based export gating.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Run summary includes status, counts, duration, validation, artifacts.
- [ ] Critical validation failures block export when policy requires.
- [ ] SQL/CSV/JSON exports are generated from one run.
- [ ] Manifest includes reproducibility and lineage fields.
- [ ] Historical runs/artifacts are queryable by authorized users.
- [ ] Failure diagnostics are actionable and sanitized.
- [ ] Export/validation verified with real file/database targets in integration tests.

title: Expose API Keys, Public REST API, and npm Package for Automation
body:
## User Story

As a platform integrator, I want API keys, a public REST API, and an npm package so that I can automate Concoction in CI/CD and developer tooling.

## Business Requirements

- API key lifecycle (create/rotate/expire/revoke) with scopes.
- Public API for workspace/project/chat/workflow/run/artifact/key operations.
- RBAC-consistent authorization across UI and API.
- Typed npm client for MVP API operations.
- API usage auditing and rate-limiting.

## Technical Requirements

- Secure key hashing/storage with scope metadata and last-used tracking.
- Define an `IApiKeyStore` application port and register secure hashing/storage adapters via DI in the composition root.
- OpenAPI-documented REST endpoints with consistent error schema.
- Pagination/idempotency support where needed.
- TypeScript npm SDK with auth helpers and long-run polling support.
- API telemetry and audit integration.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Owners can manage API keys with scoped permissions.
- [ ] Plaintext keys are only visible at creation time.
- [ ] Public endpoints support documented MVP operations.
- [ ] Invalid/expired/revoked keys are rejected consistently.
- [ ] npm client can authenticate, trigger run, poll status, and fetch artifacts.
- [ ] API usage is auditable by key scope and endpoint.
- [ ] Contract and integration tests run against full API service/runtime components.
